seed.title: FSD accident economy collapse
seed.prompt: Given the thesis that autonomy removes a large share of human-caused crashes, discover the institutions and second-order dependencies built on the crash event.
seed.thesis: A crash is not only a safety failure; it is the substrate for insurance, repair, litigation, trauma workflows, advertising, and organ-supply edges.
goals: recover accident volume as a substrate, not just a safety metric
map hidden dependents before jumping to solutions
surface non-obvious branches a human can judge for fertility

operators.label: Substrate removal
operators.description: Find the event or institutional substrate that disappears if the seed is true.

operators.label: Hidden dependent
operators.description: Follow the removed substrate into second- and third-order actors who depend on it.

operators.label: Replacement surface
operators.description: Find where pressure migrates when the old event stream dries up.

sourcePackets.title: Crash as economic event
sourcePackets.mechanism: If autonomy removes crash volume, industries that price, litigate, repair, and treat crashes lose their event stream.

candidate.title: Accident volume is the substrate
candidate.thesis: The non-obvious object is not safer cars; it is the drying-up of a recurring economic event that feeds insurance, legal, repair, medical, and media systems.

delta.summary: Moved from safety improvement to crash event stream as the load-bearing substrate.

claims: Insurance and collision repair depend on accident frequency.
The crash event feeds more systems than car service and premiums.
evidence: case statement: personal-auto insurance, plaintiff law, trauma medicine, and collision repair exist to price, litigate, repair, and treat human error
case statement: remove the error and you remove their substrate

sourcePackets.title: Insurer advertising dependency
sourcePackets.mechanism: If auto risk pools shrink, the advertising budgets used to compete for those pools lose part of their reason to exist.

candidate.title: Crash removal hits ad-supported media
candidate.thesis: Auto insurers are large advertisers, so a shrinking accident-priced insurance pool can remove demand from media inventory that appears unrelated to road safety.

delta.summary: Moved from insurer premiums to the ad markets insurers fund.

claims: Advertising demand can be downstream of accident risk.
Media may feel a crash-economy unwind before consumers notice it.
evidence: case statement: auto insurers are among the largest advertisers in America
case statement: ad-supported media is partly funded by the crash

sourcePackets.title: Trauma donor edge
sourcePackets.mechanism: If fatal crashes decline, the transplant system loses a concentrated source of high-quality donor organs.

candidate.title: Organ supply loses a concentrated source
candidate.thesis: Removing crashes can tighten the high-quality edge of organ supply because young trauma victims are disproportionately valuable donors.

delta.summary: Moved from fewer deaths to a downstream shortage in a system that depends on some trauma deaths.

claims: Some social systems depend on tragic events they would morally prefer to eliminate.
Organ supply may be affected at the concentrated edge, not just macro volume.
evidence: case statement: trauma deaths are a declining but disproportionately high-quality source of donor organs
case statement: removing young crash victims tightens the best part of transplant supply

sourcePackets.title: Liability migrates to product stack
sourcePackets.mechanism: If human fault stops being the ordinary cause, legal and insurance pressure shifts toward autonomy product liability, fleet operations, software logs, and manufacturer risk pools.

candidate.title: Fault moves from driver to stack
candidate.thesis: The plaintiff and insurance systems do not vanish cleanly; some pressure migrates from individual driver fault to autonomy stack liability and fleet-level audit trails.

delta.summary: Moved from accident-economy collapse to the replacement surface for residual harm.

claims: Residual accidents still need liability assignment.
The party exposed to claims changes when a machine is driving.
evidence: case statement: plaintiff bar and insurance depend on human error behind the wheel
autonomy changes the locus of control from driver to stack

sourcePackets.title: Car insurance gets cheaper
sourcePackets.mechanism: Stops at the first-order consumer benefit without mapping hidden dependents or replacement surfaces.
sourcePackets.noDeltaReason: No discovery delta: the case explicitly names this as the obvious first move.

sourcePackets.title: Media demand sink
sourcePackets.mechanism: If insurer ad budgets fall, local and broadcast media lose a repeat buyer category and must replace a demand sink, not just a sponsor.

candidate.title: Insurer ads are a demand sink
candidate.thesis: The advertising branch sharpens into a media-market problem: accident risk supports a repeat performance-marketing buyer that fills inventory across TV, sports, search, and local media.

delta.summary: Sharpened insurer ad spend into a demand-sink thesis for media inventory.
changes: insurer ad spend -> media demand sink

claims: The effect is not only fewer mascots; it is missing demand for ad inventory.
A shrinking risk pool changes customer-acquisition economics.
evidence: case statement: auto insurers are among the largest advertisers
case statement: ad-supported media is partly funded by the crash
